blockers.title: Corrected-email conflict resolution leaves the duplicate row live and later releasable
blockers.evidence: Spec lines 124-132 and vault Migration & Claim-Wave Plan lines 77-83 require the non-matching duplicate row to become a permanent archived conflict trail. But convex/admin/claims.ts:141-195 only patches the selected row when correctedEmail is supplied. The former duplicate left at the old email remains claim_state=conflict; after the selected row moves away, archiveConflictRow refuses it as not_duplicate at convex/admin/claims.ts:244-252, while resolveConflictAsClaimed can still release it because no other live row shares that email at convex/admin/claims.ts:171-195. The UI also still offers release for any conflict row at src/admin/ClaimConflictsQueue.tsx:131-185.
blockers.required_fix: When resolving a duplicate pair with a corrected email, either require/archive the paired row first or atomically move the other duplicate to archived_conflict. Ensure the leftover duplicate cannot later be released to unclaimed, and add a regression test for corrected-email duplicate resolution.

blockers.title: The member data-request route is not reachable for minors or restricted members
blockers.evidence: Spec lines 191-203 say the member-area Settings panel is where a signed-in member requests export or erasure of her own account. Privacy vault notes preserve data access/deletion rights for members (Privacy & Data Protection lines 58 and 74-78; Privacy Policy draft line 73). The new <YourData /> section is placed after Settings' locked early return, so locked lanes never see it: src/portal/Settings.tsx:26-35 and 170-245. Active minors do not render Settings at all; their Dashboard branch only shows youth content and sign-out at src/portal/Dashboard.tsx:140-209. Pending_guardian/pending_review members also only see waiting/sign-out states at src/portal/Dashboard.tsx:110-131.
blockers.required_fix: Expose the Your data controls independently from adult-only directory/pipeline choices. At minimum, render export/erasure request controls for every signed-in member state where the user has a session, including active minors, pending_guardian, pending_review, and restricted_unknown, while keeping adult-only toggles locked.

blockers.title: Unauthorized admin writes do not consistently return the required neutral result envelope
blockers.evidence: Spec lines 94-106 and 251-257 require admin mutations/actions to return named result envelopes, with queries allowed to throw. resolveConflictAsClaimed and approveDataRequest catch requireSuperAdmin, but decidePipelineReviewFromPanel calls requireSuperAdmin directly at convex/admin/pipelineReviews.ts:75-77 and resendGuardianEmailFromPanel calls requireSuperAdminInAction directly at convex/guardians.ts:452-464. Tests assert these throw for non-admins at tests/convex/adminPanel.test.ts:168-172 and 956-964, so the gap is intentional in the branch.
blockers.required_fix: Catch authorization failures in every public admin mutation/action and return { ok: false, error: "not_authorized" } without throwing. Update tests to assert the envelope for decidePipelineReviewFromPanel and resendGuardianEmailFromPanel.

non_blocking_notes: npm test and npm run build could not complete in this read-only review sandbox because Vite/Astro attempted to write node_modules/.vite-temp and .astro/content.d.ts.
Secret scan of the diff found only env-var names, placeholders, and test keys; no committed real secret found.
Brand string scan of changed admin/member-facing UI found no em dash/en dash and no gold token usage.

spec_compliance.criterion: 1. Admin identity check on every admin query and mutation
spec_compliance.evidence: Server-side allowlist exists in convex/lib/adminAuth.ts and is used broadly, but public admin writes in pipelineReviews and guardians throw instead of returning the required neutral envelope.
List and basic actions exist, but corrected-email duplicate resolution does not guarantee the other duplicate becomes archived_conflict and never claimable.
listPendingReviews requires super admin and surfaces consent evidence; applyPipelineDecision enforces standard lane, active lifecycle, and latest true pipeline consent before approval.
listPendingGuardians masks guardian email, and resendGuardianEmailFromPanel reuses the guardian send path without any code path that confirms consent.
Backend submitMyDataRequest exists and uses the session email, but the UI is only reachable through adult Settings and is hidden from minors/restricted/pending member states.
listDataRequests and approveDataRequest are super-admin gated; fulfilExport and executeErasure are absent, matching the spec's 2026-07-04 deferral.
ConfirmAction is shared across claim conflicts, pipeline reviews, guardians, and data requests.
listAdminAuditLog filters source=admin_fallback through the by_source_time index and paginates recent panel actions.
Changed member/admin-facing strings scan clean for em/en dashes; admin UI uses existing tokens and no gold accent.

spec_compliance.criterion: 10. No bulk export, no enumeration, neutral errors
spec_compliance.evidence: No bulk export or raw member search found, but neutral error envelopes are not consistent for admin pipeline and guardian writes.
Coverage exists, but it encodes the unauthorized-throw behavior that violates the spec; full test/build verification was blocked by read-only filesystem writes.
No Contributor, Safeguarding Deputy, certificate admin, event admin, broadcast, member-directory, or config editor surface was added.

summary: The slice has the main server-side admin gates and most queue mechanics, but it misses three acceptance/security guarantees: conflict duplicate archival is not safe, data rights controls are not reachable for protected member states, and two admin write paths violate the required neutral result envelope.
